content:
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 4, 2025 8:10PM
Daniel Escobar
Eudora Weltey uses several symbolic elements to depict her moments of perseverance and sacrifice in "A Worn Path". One example of this can be seen in the log bridge that Phoenix has to cross. The log bridge symbolizes not only an obstacle, but the fear that comes with having to overcome obstacles. This symbolizes perseverance, although she was scared, she closed her eyes and made her way across the bridge and got to the other side. Another symbol of perseverance is when she gets her dress caught in the thorn bush, eventually being able to free herself. The thorn bush represents something holding her back in life, however through sheer willpower she is able to get free and keep moving forward. "Finally, trembling all over, she stood free, and after a moment dared to stoop for her cane. “Sun so high!” she cried, leaning back and looking, while the thick tears went over her eyes." This quote perfectly captures the struggle one faces in life, as well as the reward for perseverance. She made it out of the thorn bush, and awaiting her was the Sun, or light which symbolizes in a way that there is a light at the end of the tunnel. The story also demonstrates themes
